title: Neuroloma nudicaule
intro: Neuroloma nudicaule, also known as N. nudicaule, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties, suggesting its potential in reducing oxidative stress and promoting neurological well-being. The bioactive constituents of Neuroloma nudicaule include phenolic compounds, such as ferulic acid and caffeic acid, as well as other flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Neuroloma nudicaule is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and extracts, and is sometimes combined with other herbs in supplements designed to support cognitive function and overall health.
taxonomy: Neuroloma nudicaule is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to South America. The classification of Neuroloma nudicaule is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, and clade Eudicots, as well as the clade Asterids. Within the Asteraceae family, Neuroloma nudicaule is classified as belonging to the subfamily Stifftioideae. The exact classification of Neuroloma nudicaule is still subject to ongoing research and taxonomic refinement.
